name: @shaferllc/keel
description: The house framework for Node.js — a service container, providers, routing, JSX views, and a code-generating console.

docs.title: Accounts
docs.summary: Password reset, email verification, and two-factor authentication — the flows every app with a login needs, built on primitives already in core (hash, encryption, mail, rate-limit).

docs.title: Building Keel apps with AI
docs.summary: Keel is built to be written with an AI agent.

docs.title: API Resources
docs.summary: apiResource(router, Model, options) generates a full CRUD REST API from a Keel model — explicit, server-side, and composed from pieces you already have.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.summary: Keel is small on purpose. This page maps the pieces and traces a request from socket to response. Nothing here is magic — every layer is a short, readable file in src/core/, and this guide is mostly a reading order for it.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.summary: Session-based auth built on the pieces you already have: sessions hold the login, hashing checks passwords.

docs.title: Authorization
docs.summary: Where authentication answers who you are, authorization answers what you're allowed to do.

docs.title: Billing
docs.summary: Keel Billing is a subscription-billing layer for charging customers, managing subscriptions, and reconciling gateway state through webhooks.

docs.title: Broadcasting
docs.summary: Push events to clients in real time over named channels.

docs.title: Service Broker
docs.summary: Structure an application as services that talk to each other by name instead of by import.

docs.title: Cache
docs.summary: A small cache with TTLs and the remember pattern.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.summary: All notable changes to Keel are documented here.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.summary: Keel loads configuration from two sources: environment variables (.env) and config files (config/.ts).

docs.title: The Console
docs.summary: Keel ships with a console for running the server and generating code.

docs.title: The Service Container
docs.summary: The container is the backbone of Keel. Every service — config, the router, controllers, and anything you write — is registered in it and resolved out of it. It is the single registry every service resolves out of.

docs.title: Controllers
docs.summary: Controllers are plain classes in app/Controllers/.

docs.title: CORS
docs.summary: Cross-Origin Resource Sharing lets browsers on other origins call your API.

docs.title: Database
docs.summary: Keel ships a small, driver-agnostic query builder.

docs.title: Debugging
docs.summary: Two helpers for the moments you'd otherwise reach for console.log.

docs.title: Request Decorators
docs.summary: Attach named, computed values to the current request — request.user, request.tenant, request.locale — resolved lazily and memoized for the life of the request.

docs.title: Errors & Exceptions
docs.summary: Throw an exception anywhere — a handler, middleware, or a service deep in the container — and Keel's HTTP kernel turns it into the right response.

docs.title: Events
docs.summary: A tiny event emitter for decoupling — fire an event in one place, handle it in another.

docs.title: Factories & Seeders
docs.summary: Populate the database with realistic fixtures for tests and demos.

docs.title: From install to deploy
docs.summary: One path from zero to a live Keel app — locally, on Cloudflare yourself, or on Keel Cloud with an AI agent.

docs.title: Gates
docs.summary: Keel Gates is a signup gate for private alpha / waitlist apps: an email allowlist, invite codes with use limits and expiry, and a single check that answers "may this person register?".

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.summary: Keel is a house framework for Node.js — a small, legible MVC layer over Hono.

docs.title: Hashing & Encryption
docs.summary: Password hashing and value encryption, both built on the Web Crypto API — so they run identically on Node and the edge, with no native bindings (no bcrypt to compile).

docs.title: Health Checks
docs.summary: Two endpoints, answering the two questions an orchestrator — Kubernetes, Fly, Railway, a load balancer — actually asks:

docs.title: Helpers
docs.summary: Keel gives you a handful of global helper functions so you can reach the running application from anywhere — a route handler, a model, a plain function — without threading a container reference through every call.

docs.title: Built on Hono
docs.summary: Keel's HTTP layer is Hono — an ultrafast, web-standard router that runs on Node, Cloudflare Workers, Deno, Bun, and more.

docs.title: Lifecycle Hooks
docs.summary: Tap into the application lifecycle — run code once the app is ready, clean up on shutdown, and observe route registration.

docs.title: Hosting
docs.summary: Keel Hosting is a small toolkit for hosted Workers / D1 apps: a Cloudflare REST client, hostname helpers, a SQLite-compatible SQL dump, and purpose-scoped secret encryption.

docs.title: Internationalization
docs.summary: Translations with ICU message formatting, plus the Intl formatters that go with them.

docs.title: Inertia
docs.summary: Keel ships a server-side Inertia.js adapter.

docs.title: Locks
docs.summary: "Only one of you may do this at a time" — across processes, across nodes.

docs.title: Logger
docs.summary: A small leveled logger. It writes structured JSON by default — one line per event, ready for log aggregators — and pretty single-line output in debug. Reach it with the global logger() helper.

docs.title: Mail
docs.summary: Send email through a pluggable transport. Compose a message with a fluent builder and dispatch it — the API mirrors the database layer (setMailer / mail() are to mail what setConnection / db() are to the database). The core imports no SDK: the built-in transports use fetch, console, or memory, so it runs on Node and the edge.

docs.title: Middleware
docs.summary: Middleware wraps every request, running code before and after your route handler.

docs.title: Migrations
docs.summary: Version your database schema. A migration is a { name, up, down } object; a fluent schema builder describes tables, and the migrator runs them against your connection, tracking what's applied. The SQL is dialect-aware (sqlite / mysql / postgres) and the core imports no driver.

docs.title: Models
docs.summary: Model is a tiny active-record layer over the query builder.

docs.title: Notifications
docs.summary: Send a message to a recipient over one or more channels — mail, database, or your own — inline or through the queue.

docs.title: OpenAPI
docs.summary: Keel OpenAPI generates an OpenAPI 3 spec from your routes and serves Swagger UI to explore it.

docs.title: ORM
docs.summary: Keel's ORM is a compact active record over the query builder: a model is a class pointed at a table, and its rows come back as typed objects with methods.

docs.title: Packages
docs.summary: A package is a redistributable slice of a Keel app — routes, a UI, config, migrations, console commands — that installs with a single app.register(...).

docs.title: Pages
docs.summary: Page-based routing — a file is a route.

docs.title: Service Providers
docs.summary: Service providers are the central place to configure your application.

docs.title: Query Builder
docs.summary: Keel's driver-agnostic query builder — build and run SQL by chaining methods off db(table).

docs.title: Queues & Jobs
docs.summary: Move slow work — sending mail, calling an API, processing an upload — off the request path.

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.summary: rateLimiter() is a middleware that caps how many requests a client can make in a window.

docs.title: Redis
docs.summary: A Redis integration built on a small pluggable driver — like the database and mail layers, the core imports no client, so it runs on Node and on the edge.

docs.title: Request & Response
docs.summary: Beyond the terse param() / json() shortcuts, the request and response accessors give you the full input/output surface — no context threading.

docs.title: Routing
docs.summary: Routes live in routes/web.ts. The default export receives the Router and registers routes on it. The HTTP kernel later compiles them onto Hono.

docs.title: Task Scheduling
docs.summary: Declare recurring work with a fluent cadence, then let a single cron trigger drive it — a code-defined scheduler, edge-first.

docs.title: Securing SSR apps
docs.summary: Two middlewares harden server-rendered apps: securityHeaders() sets the defensive HTTP headers browsers act on, and csrf() blocks cross-site form submissions.

docs.title: Sessions
docs.summary: Keel ships a cookie-backed session store. There's no external service to run, so it works the same on Node and on the edge. Session data lives in an HTTP-only cookie: the middleware reads it before your handler runs and writes it back afterward.

docs.title: Social authentication
docs.summary: "Sign in with GitHub / Google / Discord" — OAuth 2.0, without an SDK.

docs.title: Static Files
docs.summary: serveStatic() serves files from a directory (default public/) before your routes run.

docs.title: Storage
docs.summary: File storage over a pluggable disk — like the database and mail layers, the core imports no filesystem or SDK, so it runs on Node and the edge.

docs.title: Teams
docs.summary: Multi-tenancy, membership, roles, and invitations — where a row belongs to a team, and one team can never see another's.

docs.title: Telemetry
docs.summary: Distributed tracing — spans, W3C trace context, and an OTLP exporter — with no SDK.

docs.title: Templates
docs.summary: A string templating engine — {{ }} interpolation and @-prefixed tags for logic, includes, layouts, and components.

docs.title: Testing
docs.summary: Test your app by injecting requests — no server, no port, no network — and asserting on the response.

docs.title: Transformers
docs.summary: A model knows the database; a transformer knows the API.

docs.title: URL Builder
docs.summary: Generate URLs from named routes so paths live in one place.

docs.title: Validation
docs.summary: validate() parses request input against a schema and returns typed data.

docs.title: Views
docs.summary: Keel renders HTML with Hono JSX — type-safe components that run identically on Node and on Cloudflare Workers (no filesystem templating, so it ports anywhere).

docs.title: Vite
docs.summary: Wire a modern frontend build — bundling, hashed filenames, hot module reload — to Keel's server-rendered HTML, the way modern full-stack frameworks do.

docs.title: Watch
docs.summary: Keel Watch is a debug dashboard for Keel apps.

generators.note: A controller class resolved from the container. `-r` scaffolds all seven RESTful actions.
A service provider with register()/boot() lifecycle hooks.
A database seeder with an async run().
A queued job with an async handle().
An API transformer mapping a value to its serialized shape.
